items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Kolhapur District Central Co-op Bank Ltd., Maharashtra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated July 17, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹13.30 lakh (Rupees Thirteen Lakh Thirty Thousand only) on Kolhapur District Central Co-op Bank Ltd., Maharashtra (the bank) for non-compliance with certain provisions of section 20(1) read with section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of section 47A(1)(c) read with sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the BR Act. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by National Bank for Agriculture and

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Manmad Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Manmad, Maharashtra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated July 17, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹1 lakh (Rupees One Lakh only) on Manmad Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Manmad, Maharashtra (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Loans and Advances to Directors, their Relatives, and Firms / Concerns in which they are Interested’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of section 47A(1)(c) read with sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted b

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The United Puri-Nimapara Central Co-operative Bank Limited, Odisha
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated July 14, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹3 lakh (Rupees Three Lakh only) on The United Puri-Nimapara Central Co-operative Bank Limited, Odisha (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Know Your Customer (KYC)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of section 47A(1)(c) read with sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)

items.title: Premature redemption under Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme - Redemption Price for premature redemption of SGB 2019-20 Series-VIII due on July 21, 2026
items.summary: In terms of GOI notification F.No.4(7)-B(W&M)/2019 dated September 30, 2019 (SGB 2019-20 Series-VIII-Issue date January 21, 2020) on Sovereign Gold Bond Scheme, premature redemption of Gold Bond may be permitted after fifth year from the date of issue of such Gold Bond on the date on which interest is payable. Accordingly, the next due date of premature redemption of the above tranche shall be on July 21, 2026. 2. Further, the redemption price of SGB shall be based on simple average of closing price of gold of 999 purity of previous three business days from the date of redemption, as published

items.title: Auction of Government of India Dated Securities
items.summary: Government of India (GoI) has announced the sale (issue / re-issue) of two dated securities for a notified amount of ₹28,000 crore as per the following details: Sr No Security Date of Repayment Notified Amount (₹ Crore) GoI specific Notification Auction Date Settlement Date 1 New GS 2041 Jul 27, 2041 17,000 F.No.4(1)-B(W&M)/2026 dated July 20, 2026 July 24, 2026 (Friday) July 27, 2026 (Monday) 2 7.43% GS 2076 Jan 19, 2076 11,000 Total 28,000 2. GoI will have the option to retain additional subscription up to ₹2,000 crore against each security mentioned above. 3. The securities will be sold thr

items.title: Reporting of FCNR(B) Deposits, External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs) and Overseas Foreign Currency Borrowings (OFCBs) mobilized under Reserve Bank’s Swap Facility
items.summary: With a view to strengthen our balance of payments and incentivise capital inflows, RBI had announced a series of measures including a facility for offering concessional swaps for fresh FCNR(B) deposits, OFCB and ECB inflows, on June 5, 2026. This facility was operationalized on June 8, 2026, and is available upto September 30, 2026, for the FCNR(B) deposits and upto December 31, 2026, for the OFCBs and ECBs. 2. The swap facility has seen avid interest and attracted steady forex inflows since June 8, 2026. Based on the data received from Authorised Dealer Banks, the position of forex inflows mo

items.title: RBI to conduct 3-day Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction under LAF on July 21, 2026
items.summary: On a review of current and evolving liquidity conditions, it has been decided to conduct a Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction on Tuesday, July 21, 2026, as under: Sl. No. Notified Amount (₹ crore) Tenor (day) Window Timing Date of Reversal 1 75,000 3 09:30 AM to 10:00 AM July 24, 2026 (Friday) 2. The operational guidelines for the auction will be same as given in Reserve Bank’s Press Release 2021-2022/1572 dated January 20, 2022 . Ajit Prasad Deputy General Manager (Communications) Press Release: 2026-2027/721

items.title: Alphabet stock pops on report it's developing a more efficient AI chip
items.summary: The new AI chip, called "Frozen v2," would embed parts of Gemini's architecture directly into the silicon, according to the report.

items.title: Ryanair says 'no shortage' of travelers despite Iran war denting profit
items.summary: Ryanair's first-quarter profit took a 34% hit as consumers delayed bookings due to the Middle East crisis, while struggling airlines face a "difficult winter."

items.title: Cybersecurity risks posed by over-the-air tech in autos has analysts concerned
items.summary: The automotive industry's increasing use of over-the-air technology makes it more susceptible to cyberattacks, analysts say.

items.title: U.S. death toll rises again as Iran war escalates over the weekend
items.summary: Centcom says it hit Iran's coastal surveillance and air defense facilities a day after two U.S. troops were killed in an Iranian strike on Jordan.

items.title: Oil prices erase gains after Iran says U.S. talks could be pursued based on national interests
items.summary: American forces have started the ninth consecutive night of strikes against Iranian targets.

items.title: Govt sees good response to pre-application conference for Rs 37,500-cr coal gasification scheme
items.summary: The government on Monday held a pre-application conference for its ₹37,500 crore scheme to promote surface coal gasification projects, drawing strong interest from public sector firms, private companies, technology providers and prospective applicants. Officials clarified eligibility criteria, financial incentives and the application process under the scheme.

items.title: UK's Andy Burnham becomes PM as Trump slams Britain as 'Poverty Stricken Disaster'
items.summary: The prospect of Burnham, considered more left-leaning than Starmer, replacing the incumbent prime minister sent jitters through bond markets earlier this year.

items.title: AMD launches Helios, its first rack AI system to rival Nvidia, adding Microsoft as newest buyer
items.summary: AMD is taking aim at Nvidia with Helios, its first AI rack system, and has landed Microsoft as a new customer, joining Meta, OpenAI and Oracle.

items.title: Steptrade’s pre-IPO fund secures Rs 100 crore in first close
items.summary: Steptrade Capital has raised over Rs 100 crore in the first close of its Rs 500 crore Chanakya Opportunities Fund II. The Category II AIF will invest in growth-stage pre-IPO companies across manufacturing, energy transition and emerging technology sectors, beginning deployments in August.

items.title: India has adequate coal stocks to meet thermal power demand, with 42.8 MT available: Power Ministry
items.summary: India has sufficient coal stocks to meet electricity demand, with 42.8 million tonnes available at thermal power plants as of July 12, enough for 14 days at 85% plant load factor, the government informed the Rajya Sabha. Coal-based plants, with a capacity of 230.8 GW, supplied nearly 70% of the country's electricity between April and June, while coal supplies are being monitored daily by an inter-ministerial committee.

items.title: IIT Madras to reap ₹112 crore from Indo-MIM IPO
items.summary: Indo-MIM plans to raise ₹3,812 crore through its mainboard IPO, price band fixed at ₹461-485 per equity share

items.title: Indian Overseas Bank eyes $650 million foreign deposits by September
items.summary: Indian Overseas Bank aims to gather $650 million in foreign currency deposits by September. The bank has already secured $300 million without utilizing leverage options. Overall, the lender plans to raise one billion dollars through deposits and external borrowing. This projection follows a significant 49.3% jump in first quarter net profit. The bank also reported strong growth in advances and net interest income.

items.title: US stocks rebound as chipmakers recover, investors brace for big tech earnings
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.02%, the S&P 500 rose 0.42%, the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.79%

items.title: Boeing CEO says company still needs 'a couple more years' to repair finances before new jet
items.summary: CEO Kelly Ortberg signalled Boeing is focused more on stabilizing its existing business than rushing to develop a successor to its bestselling 737 MAX.

items.title: Caliber Mining IPO subscribed 23.37 times on day 2
items.summary: The IPO has a fresh issue of equity shares worth ₹400 crore and an offer-for-sale of shares worth ₹50 crore by promoters

items.title: Nearly 55.49 crore UPI users onboarded by June 2026
items.summary: Around 55.49 crore UPI users were onboarded by June 2026, Parliament was informed. UPI transactions reached 24,162 crore in volume and Rs 314 lakh crore in value. NPCI International Payments is expanding UPI and RuPay to global markets. Security measures and international linkages enhance UPI's safety and reach. Government and regulators address fintech ecosystem issues and consumer protection.

items.title: Rupee hits two-month low as oil nears $90 amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: The rupee settled at 96.4450 per dollar after hitting a two-month low, with markets tracking oil prices, Middle East tensions and interest rate expectations

items.title: No proposal at present to scrap LTCG on equity transactions: Finance Ministry
items.summary: Government revenue from LTCG tax increased nearly 78% to ₹1.29 lakh crore in FY25

items.title: Aastha Spintex board to consider bonus, dividend
items.summary: Board to consider capex for expansion of forward integrated textile products

items.title: Sovereign Gold Bond investors earn up to 44% despite gold price correction
items.summary: SGBs maturing up to 2032 have delivered 34-44% returns over the past year, though recent tax changes have reduced their appeal for fresh investors

items.title: No plan to remove LTCG tax on equity investments, Finance Ministry tells Parliament
items.summary: The clarification follows last month’s decision to exempt FPIs from LTCG tax on government securities to attract long-term foreign capital

items.title: Oil touches $90 after Iran hits tankers
items.summary: Crude later falls back after Tehran says it has received proposals from mediators about the war

items.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods
items.summary: The Chinese retailer allowed unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, says the EU.

items.title: India has adequate coal stocks to meet power demand, says government
items.summary: The government said coal supplies are being monitored daily as thermal power and energy storage capacity continue to expand

items.title: IndiGo selects Honeywell Aerospace's avionics, power systems for 810 A320 neo family planes
items.summary: IndiGo has selected Honeywell Aerospace's avionics and power systems for 810 Airbus A320neo family aircraft scheduled for delivery in the coming years. Honeywell said the agreement is the largest new-aircraft selectable equipment contract in its history and will cover auxiliary power units, weather radar, collision avoidance systems, flight management systems, and aftermarket support.

items.title: Exits from gold ETFs matched inflows last week as the precious metal hovers near $4,000/oz
items.summary: Every dollar inflow into physically-backed gold exchange-traded funds (ETFs) was matched by a similar outflow last week, shows WGC data

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks open higher as chips recover, megacap earnings loom
items.summary: Wall Street opened higher as chip stocks rebounded from last week’s sharp decline, supporting gains across major indexes. Investors are now focused on upcoming earnings from leading technology companies driving the AI rally, with sentiment stabilising despite recent volatility and ongoing uncertainty around interest rates and global risks.

items.title: Pilots' grouping ALPA India seeks suspension of Indian airlines' flights to West Asia
items.summary: In March this year also, the Airline Pilots' Association of India (ALPA India) had asked the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) to adopt a precautionary approach in view of the evolving military situation and the increasing risks to civil aviation in West Asia.

items.title: Sobha Ltd Q1 Results: Profit jumps over 3-fold to Rs 51 crore, revenue rises to Rs 1,330 crore
items.summary: Sobha Ltd experienced remarkable growth in net profit for the June quarter, seeing a threefold increase compared to last year. Significant gains in total income were reported, alongside record-breaking quarterly sales attributed to new project launches. A striking seventy-six percent rise in sales bookings reflects robust housing demand, positioning Sobha Ltd to maintain this upward momentum with a promising project pipeline.

items.title: Indian banks mobilise $20.7 billion under RBI's special incentive window
items.summary: In a remarkable financial boost, India's central bank recorded significant foreign exchange inflows of $20.72 billion, primarily through a swap facility provided by the Reserve Bank of India. Out of this amount, Foreign Currency Non-Resident Deposits accounted for $17.41 billion. Meanwhile, External Commercial Borrowings contributed $1.34 billion, and Overseas Foreign Currency Borrowings brought in an additional $1.97 billion, enhancing the country’s economic standing.

items.title: U.S. Treasury yields edge higher as Wall Street monitors Middle East tensions
items.summary: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — rose over 1 basis point to 4.558%.

items.title: Q1 Results Today Live: UltraTech PAT up, IOB, KVB, Sobha Q1 PAT climb, Mahindra Logistics, Dynamic Cables & Shyam Metalics zoom, Paytm, JP Power, Bluestone to announce Q1 results
items.summary: Q1 Results Today, 20th July 2026 Live Updates:

items.title: US stocks to buy for short term: From Nvidia to Netflix- Appreciate CEO suggests picking these 5 shares
items.summary: Appreciate CEO Subho Moulik reveals five key stocks to watch. From Nvidia's AI dominance to Netflix's innovative revenue strategies, these companies showcase how the evolving landscape of technology is shaping investment opportunities.

items.title: Broker’s call: WeWork India (Buy)
items.summary: ICICI Securities

items.title: Voters wary of government ownership in companies as Trump administration takes equity stakes
items.summary: About half of voters believe it isn't appropriate for the U.S. government to own stakes in companies, a new CNBC poll found.

items.title: Broker’s call: Northern Arc (Buy)
items.summary: Motilal Oswal

items.title: WABAG bags ₹250–600 crore BWSSB order for Bengaluru sewage treatment plants
items.summary: The contract covers Design, Build and Operate (DBO) of a 100 MLD Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) and a 25 MLD Tertiary Treatment Plant (TTP) at Byramangala, and a 60 MLD STP at Bellandur

items.title: Suzlon Energy board to consider Q1FY27 results on July 28; shares down 10% in July
items.summary: Suzlon Energy's Board of Directors will meet on July 28 to approve Q1FY27 financial results. The company reported a 5.6% decline in consolidated net profit for March 2026, despite rising operational revenue.

items.title: Emcure Pharma gets CDSCO nod to market Poviztra for fatty liver treatment
items.summary: Emcure Pharmaceuticals announced its brand Poviztra is now available for fatty liver treatment. This follows regulatory approval for treating metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis in adults. Poviztra contains innovator semaglutide manufactured in Europe by Novo Nordisk. The approval expands access to treatment for this progressive liver disease. This marks a significant milestone for patients with this serious condition.

items.title: Burnham promises help with your money. How could it affect you?
items.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham says he wants life to be more affordable, but how could he do it?

items.title: JSW Steel shares gain as Q1 earnings boost investor sentiment
items.summary: Brokerages remain constructive on long-term growth outlook

items.title: Silverstorm Parks and Resorts to raise ₹82 cr via IPO on BSE SME platform
items.summary: Silverstorm Parks and Resorts to issue 61.98 lakh equity shares in the price band of ₹123-₹133 a share

items.title: The Titans of Nasdaq 100: Top companies ranked by market cap
items.summary: The Nasdaq-100’s largest companies continue to dominate global technology, AI, cloud computing and digital markets. Nvidia and Apple lead by market value, while Amazon tops revenue, reflecting the scale, innovation and financial strength driving the world’s biggest technology businesses.

items.title: Sobha plans to raise up to Rs1,000 cr through non-convertible debentures
items.summary: Sobha Ltd's board approved raising up to Rs 1,000 crore through privately placed non-convertible debentures in one or more tranches. The fund raise will enhance financing flexibility, with commercial terms to be finalised later by the investments and borrowings committee.

items.title: Expert view: Overweight on diversified financials, automobiles, new age tech firms, says Nandish Shah of Motilal Oswal
items.summary: Expert view: Nandish Shah, AVP-PCG Research and Advisory, (Fundamental) Wealth Management, Motilal Oswal Financial Services, is overweight on diversified financials, automobiles, new age tech platforms, manufacturing and industrials, and consumer discretionary sectors.

items.title: Downfall of a Wall Street darling: Why Oracle shares crashed 65% from peak
items.summary: Oracle shares have dropped about 65% from their peak as investors shift focus from AI-driven cloud growth to rising capital expenditure, debt and execution risks. Despite strong OCI revenue and a massive backlog, markets seek proof that AI investments can generate sustainable returns.

items.title: US stock market today: Wall Street futures gain as easing oil prices offset lingering Middle East tensions
items.summary: US stock futures rose on 20 July as oil prices fell amid renewed US-Iran diplomatic efforts, easing supply concerns. The S&P 500, Nasdaq-100, and Dow Jones futures gained, though geopolitical tensions in the Middle East persisted, affecting global markets and chip stocks.

items.title: 'Give me my money back': South Korean traders' leveraged SK Hynix, Samsung bets unravel after sell-off
items.summary: South Korean retail investors suffer heavy losses after Samsung and SK Hynix shares reverse sharply.

items.title: TSMC is accelerating Arizona factory build-out to capitalize on AI 'megatrend,' CFO says
items.summary: Speaking in an interview with CNBC, TSMC's Wendell Huang said the fresh investment comes on the back of robust customer demand.

items.title: Credit-deposit gap to persist as loan demand dynamics have changed, says SBI Research
items.summary: A noticeable gap between credit and deposit growth is troubling the banking landscape. Consumers are shifting their savings from traditional deposits to diverse financial options. Amid geopolitical uncertainties and rising oil costs, companies are requesting more working capital loans. To alleviate this funding pressure, newly introduced FCNR(B) deposits could provide a short-term remedy. It is projected that savings will increasingly gravitate towards the five-year maturity category.

items.title: Quote of the day by John Bogle: "Sometimes common sense tells us what statistics cannot"
items.summary: Legendary investor John C. Bogle stressed common sense in investing. Statistics reveal trends, but not all market dynamics or psychology. His wisdom guides investors through volatile market conditions and speculation. Bogle advocated for diversification, patience, and low costs for wealth creation. This timeless philosophy combines analysis with practical judgment for steady growth.

items.title: Alibaba’s AliExpress gets record €550 million EU fine over illegal product sales; Chinese firm to fight back
items.summary: The penalty follows an investigation launched in March 2024 under the European Union's Digital Services Act

items.title: HPCL invites LNG suppliers for spot and long-term import deals
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corp has invited LNG suppliers to register for future deliveries. The company seeks both spot purchases and long-term supply agreements. HPCL operates an LNG import facility with significant annual capacity. India aims to increase gas's share in its energy mix. This move supports the nation's goal to reduce its carbon footprint.

items.title: Why online masterclasses are replacing the executive MBA?
items.summary: The traditional Executive MBA is losing its dominance as learning needs change. New platforms offer short, practitioner-led programs for busy professionals. These programs provide direct access to experts who have solved real business problems. This shift reflects how quickly business knowledge now becomes outdated. Indian executives are redefining continuous learning for their evolving careers.

items.title: Meghna Infracon Infrastructure expects Rs 300cr revenue from new commercial project
items.summary: Meghna Infracon Infrastructure Ltd is embarking on an exciting journey with its new commercial project, Meghna One, nestled in Wagle Estate, Thane. This expansive development will feature a blend of premium office, retail, and hospitality venues, covering a substantial area of 2.8 lakh square feet. With high expectations, the company predicts a lucrative revenue of Rs 300 crore from this initiative.

items.title: India bonds steady after swinging with oil
items.summary: On Monday, Indian government bonds remained stable as crude oil prices dipped, driven by renewed optimism surrounding U.S.-Iran negotiations. The benchmark 2036 bond yield held steady, mirroring previous trading patterns. Foreign investors returned to the Indian bond market, seizing opportunities at elevated yields following earlier sell-offs. However, lingering concerns about the U.S.-Iran situation and a potential monsoon deficit continue to weigh on investor sentiment.

items.title: Eleven Power commits to invest over Rs 4,700 crore for distribution network
items.summary: Eleven Power has proposed investing over ₹4,700 crore to build a renewable-led electricity distribution network in Gurugram and Nuh, after applying for a parallel distribution licence from the Haryana Electricity Regulatory Commission. The company said funding for the first phase is fully secured, but the project depends on regulatory approval.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Today | Live: US stocks open higher as chip stocks rebound ahead of tech earnings
Bonus bonanza! Aastha Spintex to consider bonus issue, dividend less than a month since debut on July 23
items.summary: Aastha Spintex will consider a 1:1 bonus issue, a final FY26 dividend of up to Rs 10 per share, and textile expansion plans on July 23. Despite shares falling over 22% below the IPO price, the company aims to strengthen long-term growth through forward integration.

items.title: 'Stock market looking more like gambling:' Here's a Warren Buffett warning to investors
items.summary: Warren Buffett has cautioned that parts of the stock market increasingly resemble gambling rather than investing, as retail traders, AI-driven optimism and speculative options activity fuel Wall Street's rally. The Berkshire Hathaway chairman urged investors to focus on long-term value instead of chasing quick gains amid rising market exuberance.

items.title: Karnataka HC dismisses PIL against 7,400-acre Bidadi township land acquisition
items.summary: In a recent ruling, the High Court dismissed a challenge against land acquisition for a Bengaluru township, reinforcing that it cannot interfere with the government's policy choices. Chief Minister DK Shivakumar refrained from making any statements regarding the verdict. In light of farmer protests, a committee is set to review the proposed project, while the government guarantees that the land status of unwilling farmers will not be affected.

items.title: Private banks weigh on indices; PSU banks, pharma limit losses
items.summary: The Sensex fell 443 points and the Nifty lost 95 points as investors awaited more corporate earnings and the US Federal Reserve’s policy decision

items.title: No decision yet on petrol blending beyond 20% ethanol, says minister
items.summary: The government has not decided to increase ethanol blending in petrol beyond twenty percent. Any future increase will follow scientific studies and industry consultations. India achieved the twenty percent blending target five years ahead of schedule. This program has saved foreign exchange and reduced carbon emissions significantly.

items.title: Aastha Spintex sets board meeting date to consider and approve 1:1 bonus shares, 100% final dividend
items.summary: Aastha Spintex's board will meet on July 23 to discuss a bonus issue and a final dividend for shareholders. The proposed bonus involves issuing new shares and reflects confidence in the company's growth following an acquisition to expand spinning capacity.

items.title: Rhitik Jassar launches ZTA Infrastructure eyes expansion across educational, housing and institutional projects
items.summary: ZTA Group has established ZTA Infrastructure Pvt. Ltd. as its primary construction entity. This new company will lead diverse projects across India's growing infrastructure sector. ZTA Infrastructure aims to build a strong project pipeline and institutional scale. The company plans to attract strategic investors for accelerated future growth. Ultimately, ZTA Infrastructure aspires to become a publicly listed company.

items.title: HDFC Bank CEO reappointment delayed by board review, sources say
items.summary: HDFC Bank has delayed recommending CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan's reappointment to the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) as its independent directors conduct an additional review, Reuters reported citing sources. The review is examining media reports alleging the bank offered preferential rates on certain large deposits, though sources said no wrongdoing has been found so far. The board is expected to decide after the review concludes, likely in early August.

items.title: India's core sector output grows 5% in June under new base year series, hits 5-month high
items.summary: June 2026 marked a notable five percent growth in India's core industries index, with iron ore production soaring to forty-three point nine percent. Significant growth was also observed in cement and electricity. Conversely, natural gas and crude oil outputs faced reductions. For the April-June 2026 timeframe, the total growth measured at three point six percent.

items.title: IVPA seeks policy review as refined edible oil imports from Nepal breach 8 lakh tonnes
items.summary: Imports from Nepal increased from 47,295 tonnes in 2023 to 1.24 lakh tonnes (lt) in 2024, before surging to over 8.04 lt in 2025

items.title: Sona BLW Precision among 6 midcap stocks that hit 52-week highs, rally up to 20% in a month
items.summary: Despite a weak market, six BSE 150 MidCap stocks, including Sona BLW Precision and Gujarat Fluorochemicals, hit fresh 52-week highs, gaining up to 20% over the past month.

items.title: Market wrap: Trent, Axis Bank among top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
items.summary: Indian equities ended lower as escalating Iran-US tensions and surging crude prices dampened investor sentiment despite easing volatility. Banking stocks dragged benchmarks, while analysts expect geopolitical risks, inflation concerns and Q1 earnings momentum to shape market direction, with Nifty support at 24,100 and resistance near 24,400.

items.title: Sobha Q1 Results: Revenue rises 50%, profit more than triples as margins improve
items.summary: Sobha Ltd. reported a strong June 2026 quarter with a 50% year-on-year revenue rise to ₹1,278.2 crore and a net profit tripling to ₹50.8 crore. Despite a 35.7% sequential revenue decline, operating margins improved significantly.

items.title: SC gives Parsvnath Developers one week to deposit dues to homebuyers, warns next step is jail
items.summary: The Supreme Court gave Parsvnath Developers one final week to deposit homebuyers' dues. This deposit must include the entire amount along with twelve percent interest. Failure to comply will result in imprisonment for the company's directors. The court expressed strong displeasure with the real estate firm's actions. This order follows a twenty-year struggle by senior citizens for their homes.

items.title: Rupee hits two-month low, oil prices remain focal point
items.summary: The Indian rupee hit a two-month low on Monday as oil prices hovered around ninety dollars per barrel. In an effort to stabilize the currency, the Reserve Bank of India intervened by unloading dollars into the market. Meanwhile, Asian currencies and stock performances were varied, with the dollar index holding steady. This rise in energy prices could have implications for the Federal Reserve's future interest rate strategies.

items.title: India says no proposal to offer long-term tax relief for domestic equity investors
items.summary: India's finance ministry confirmed no plan to remove equity LTCG tax. This clarification follows recent tax easing for foreign investors in government debt. Domestic investors will continue paying a 12.5% LTCG tax on qualifying equity gains. The exemption for foreign portfolio investors applies to government securities only. This move aims to attract stable, long-term foreign capital into India.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex drops 442 pts to end at 77,708, Nifty closes below 24,240; Axis Bank, HDFC Bank fall most
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: The 30-share BSE Sensex declined 442.93 points, or 0.57 per cent, to settle at 77,708.52. The 50-share NSE Nifty edged lower by 95.80 points, or 0.39 per cent, to end at 24,238.50.

items.title: Lagging on FCNR(B) deposit collections, banks pin hopes on September-end rush
items.summary: Banks anticipate a surge in FCNR-B deposits before the September deadline. Initial inflows have been below expectations, but partnerships are being forged. Demand from non-resident Indians remains strong, especially from the Middle East. Lenders are intensifying outreach efforts to attract more overseas funds. Mobilisation is expected to accelerate closer to the scheme's conclusion.

items.title: Corporate loan growth outpaces retail advances at top private banks
items.summary: Corporate lending at top Indian private banks surged significantly in the June quarter. This corporate loan growth far outpaced retail credit expansion, reaching 26% on average. Higher bond yields pushed companies back to mainstream lenders for working capital needs. This revival lifted overall bank credit growth to its fastest pace in over two years. Sectors like commercial real estate and infrastructure saw increased demand for loans.

items.title: Absolute Projects, Jindal Supreme secure Sebi's nod to float IPOs
items.summary: Funds will be used to modernise Manufacturing Facility I, procure machinery for Manufacturing Facility II, and secure equipment for Power EPC projects.

items.title: ICICI Bank gains while HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra, Yes Bank decline after Q1 results
items.summary: In today’s session, ICICI Bank shares rose after reporting healthy earnings growth backed by higher net interest income, non-interest income and lower provisions

items.title: Turtlemint shares end 6% higher as company turns profitable in Q4
items.summary: For FY26, revenue rose 57 per cent y-o-y to ₹1,098 crore

items.title: India's EXIM Bank plans $300 million dollar bond sale under RBI hedging facility
items.summary: The state-run export finance institution will issue three-year floating-rate ‌bonds priced ⁠at 90 ⁠basis points over the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR), according to the bankers, ​who requested anonymity as they're not authorised to speak to the media.

items.title: Supreme Court to direct CBSE to implement APAAR consent form changes across India
items.summary: In a significant move, the Supreme Court will mandate the CBSE to abide by an Orissa High Court decision that requires revisions to the APAAR ID consent form. Parents will now have the autonomy to refuse consent or choose to opt out. The court's endorsement of the December 2025 ruling emphasizes the importance of consent and the safeguarding of data privacy.

items.title: Bonds unsettled as oil and gas climb, earnings loom for AI
items.summary: That sent U.S. yields higher, with ​the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield at 4.55%, while yields on 30-year Treasuries are back above the ⁠psychological 5.0% ‌barrier.

items.title: British government bond yields edge higher as Burnham becomes PM
items.summary: BRITAIN-BONDS/British government bond yields edge higher as Burnham becomes PM

items.title: Mrs Bectors Food shares surge 25% in 3 days after Sunil Singhania's Abakkus picks up stake
items.summary: Mrs Bectors Food Specialities shares surged on Monday, extending their three-day gains to over 25% after Sunil Singhania's Abakkus Investment Managers acquired a nearly 1% stake through a bulk deal. Investors cheered the marquee investment even as the company awaits its June quarter earnings announcement.

items.title: Smartworks leases 1,100 desks in Pune to L&T Technology Services for Rs 55cr rent in 5 years
items.summary: Smartworks Coworking Spaces has leased over 1,100 desks to L&T Technology Services in Pune. This expansion brings the total seats provided to L&T Technology Services to over 2,750. The company anticipates a rental income of Rs 55 crore over a five-year period. Enterprise workplace decisions are increasingly driven by flexibility and speed. Smartworks manages 16.1 million square feet across 66 centers in India and Singapore.

items.title: ​Multibagger smallcaps: FIIs raised stakes in these 14 stocks for 2 straight quarters; shares soared up to 450%
items.summary: Foreign institutional investors raised stakes in these smallcap stocks for two straight quarters, and the strategy paid off as select names delivered multibagger returns of up to 450% in six months.

items.title: Som Distilleries shares jump 14% as ace investor Prashant Jain buys 25 lakh shares in Q1. What is he seeing?
items.summary: Shares of Som Distilleries surged after veteran investor Prashant Jain acquired a 1.22% stake in the company during the June quarter, according to the latest shareholding data. The investment boosted investor sentiment despite the liquor maker reporting weak earnings in the previous quarter.

items.title: Ryanair profits drop as Iran war puts off passengers and lifts fuel costs
items.summary: The Irish airline said oil prices had increased costs meanwhile Brent crude surpassed $90.

items.title: Is Korean stock market turning into open casino? How retail leverage is fueling wild swings
items.summary: Memory chip bellwethers SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics make up just over half of the benchmark Kospi. Much of the stock market’s previous rally was related to the sharp surge in the share prices of these two chipmakers. These two stocks have now crashed more than 30% each in just a month, leading to the sharp downtrend in the market.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 20 July: Axis Bank, HDFC Bank, Ceat, India Cements, OLA, HFCL among top losers
items.summary: The Indian stock market faced pressure on 20 July as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment. Rising crude prices stoked fears of tighter monetary policies, while mixed sector performances were seen. Despite losses, heavyweight stocks helped Nifty 50 end down 0.40%.

items.title: India's net FDI inflows likely to reach $15 bn in FY27
items.summary: India's net FDI inflows are expected to rebound to $15 billion in fiscal year 2027. This rebound will be supported by healthy gross inflows and moderating repatriation outflows. Policy measures and a nuanced FDI framework are also expected to boost foreign investment. Outbound FDI from India is also projected to remain healthy throughout the fiscal year. These improvements signal a positive outlook for India's capital account.

items.title: Global customers are watching closely, says Skyroot founder Pawan Chandana
items.summary: This is a global milestone achieved by an Indian private company, and it demonstrates to the entire ecosystem that building world-class space technology in India is possible, Skyroot founder Pawan Chandana said

items.title: Indian Overseas Bank share price rise 3% after Q1 results 2026. Do you own?
items.summary: IOB share price opened at ₹34.14 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹33.81 on Friday last week.

items.title: Bezos backs CuspAI as startup teams up with Nvidia to hunt for chipmaking materials
items.summary: CuspAI is part of a new wave of companies looking to use AI to solve challenges in the physical world.

items.title: Lohia Corp's ₹1,102 cr IPO to open on July 23; price band at ₹404-425 per share
items.summary: The IPO is entirely an OFS of up to 2.59 crore equity shares by existing shareholders

items.title: Ethanol ATMs coming soon: The biofuel story is racing ahead of E20
items.summary: India's ethanol policy is entering a new phase as the government explores ethanol ATMs, ethanol cooking fuel, sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), exports and flex-fuel vehicles after achieving nationwide E20 petrol rollout. With ethanol production capacity outpacing current demand, policymakers are seeking new markets to absorb surplus capacity and expand India's biofuel ecosystem.

items.title: No proposal under consideration to scrap LTCG tax on equities, government clarifies
items.summary: The central government clarifies that it has no plans to abolish the long-term capital gains tax on equity transactions for retail and domestic investors, quashing market speculation. Details here.

items.title: FAA says certification of Boeing 737 MAX 7, 10 expected soon
items.summary: The Federal Aviation Administration anticipates certifying Boeing's 737 MAX 7 and 10 models soon. Deputy Administrator Chris Rocheleau stated the MAX 7 certification is imminent. He also indicated the larger MAX 10 variant will follow closely behind. The Boeing 777x aircraft is expected to receive certification after these MAX planes.

items.title: Gold futures rise to ₹1,41,200/10 gm on spot demand
Riyadh Air orders 34 Boeing, Airbus widebody jets in expansion push
items.summary: Riyadh Air has ordered thirty-four widebody aircraft from Boeing and Airbus. The airline will receive twenty-eight Boeing 787 Dreamliners and six Airbus A350-1000s. These new planes will help Riyadh Air reach over one hundred destinations by 2030. The carrier is expanding operations to establish Riyadh as a major global hub. This move supports Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 economic diversification strategy.

items.title: ISM 2.0: Can semiconductor stocks become India's next long-term wealth creators?
items.summary: The Union Cabinet had approved ₹1.27 lakh crore for the second edition of India Semiconductor Mission (ISM 2.0). At the current juncture, India offers an emerging manufacturing and infrastructure story backed by policy support.

items.title: PNB shares jump 5.5% to 2-month high after Q1 profit more than triples; brokerages turn bullish
items.summary: Punjab National Bank shares surged 5.6% to ₹111.70, driven by a 214% net profit increase to ₹5,253 crore for Q1FY27, supported by stronger operations and improved asset quality, reflecting a favorable earnings outlook.

items.title: India’s edible oil industry flags 17-fold surge in Nepal imports, seeks policy review
items.summary: In a pressing appeal, the Indian Vegetable Oil Producers' Association urges a swift revision of India's edible oil import regulations. Recent years have seen a surge in duty-free refined oil entering from Nepal, which has moved significant refining operations beyond India's borders, causing negative repercussions for local industries and fiscal health.

items.title: Jobs, borrowing and taxes: Andy Burnham's economic challenges
items.summary: The person in charge of the country will change but the financial challenges remain the same.

items.title: UltraTech Cement Q1 Results: Cons profit jumps 17% YoY to Rs 2,599 crore; revenue rises 16%
items.summary: UltraTech Cement Q1 Results: UltraTech reported a 17% YoY rise in Q1FY27 consolidated net profit to Rs 2,599 crore, while revenue increased 16% to Rs 24,648 crore. EBITDA grew 12% to Rs 5,146 crore, driven by higher cement volumes, improved realisations and strong growth in ready-mix concrete and construction chemicals. Consolidated grey cement volumes rose 12% to 41.31 million tonnes.

items.title: UltraTech Cement Q1 Results: Net profit jumps 17% YoY to ₹2,599 crore
items.summary: In Q1FY27, UltraTech Cement's net profit surged 16.77% year-on-year to ₹2,599.28 crore, driven by operational efficiencies and market execution. Revenue grew 15.8% to ₹24,648 crore, while capacity utilisation stood at 81%, indicating strong demand in the construction sector.

items.title: Axis Bank share price dips over 6% after Q1FY27 results
items.summary: Axis Bank shares fell nearly 6% due to concerns over net interest margins despite a strong profit growth. The bank reported a consolidated net profit increase of 22.23% for the June quarter, yet asset quality showed a slight decline in non-performing assets.

items.title: Global Market: Euro Zone firms see slower price and wage growth, ECB survey shows
items.summary: Euro zone companies expect slower growth in selling prices and wages over the next year, according to the ECB's latest survey, signalling that inflationary pressures remain contained despite higher energy costs. The findings strengthen expectations that the central bank may keep interest rates unchanged this week.

items.title: China's car market heads for worst year since 2021 as sales plunge 20%

items.summary: South Korea's Samsung Biologics said on Monday that it had made an all-cash bid of 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion) for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group.

items.title: Why brands need an ecosystem, not just an advertising plan
items.summary: Modern marketing is no longer won through isolated campaigns. As business audiences engage with brands across articles, events, thought leadership and digital experiences, organisations are discovering that connected ecosystems create stronger influence than standalone advertising. Partner with The Economic Times to become part of India's most influential business ecosystem.

items.title: Why Sterling Holidays is moving beyond holiday memberships before its standalone listing

items.summary: JioStar will launch six Bigg Boss editions across India in September 2026. Salman Khan and Sourav Ganguly will host popular reality show versions. The franchise reached over 500 million viewers and generated significant engagement in 2025. This expansion marks the show's biggest multi-language rollout in its history. Brands participating in the show saw notable gains in awareness and intent.

items.title: L&T bags orders worth Rs 10,000–Rs 15,000 cr in metals and minerals business
items.summary: Larsen & Toubro's Metals & Minerals business secured multiple mega orders. These contracts are from leading domestic public and private sector companies. One order involves an 18-MTPA iron ore handling plant in Chhattisgarh. Another project expands a steel plant in West Bengal significantly. L&T also won an EPC order for a new zinc processing plant.

items.title: India-UK trade deal: More British brands are coming to India, but here's why your shopping bill may not fall anytime soon
items.summary: India-UK CETA is expected to bring more British brands to India, though immediate price cuts remain unlikely due to a weaker rupee and higher shipping costs. Some brands, including Lush, have paused planned price hikes and introduced selective discounts, while broader price benefits are expected as tariffs are gradually reduced.

items.title: Relieved that crude has finally fallen? The real warning signs just began flashing elsewhere
items.summary: As the Iran crisis enters ​its fifth month, traders have become increasingly convinced that Trump will do almost anything to avoid a politically damaging spike in U.S. fuel prices. But the bright flashing warning signs coming out of the refining system suggest the U.S. president may struggle to prevent one.

items.summary: Lohia Corp IPO price band is set at ₹404-425 per share, with subscription opening on 23 July and closing on 27 July. The offering is an OFS of over 2.59 crore shares, with no fresh issue. Listing is expected on 30 July.

items.title: How rural India is bearing the brunt of the inflation rise
items.summary: India’s rural inflation has moved closer to 5%, while the urban rate remains below 4%. The gap is usually explained by higher food prices, but the latest data suggest price pressures may be spreading beyond food.

items.summary: The Mukesh Ambani-led stock Reliance Industries opened at ₹1,317 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹1,327.

items.title: A yo-yo monsoon is threatening India's crops and the power grid
items.summary: India's monsoon rainfall is becoming more erratic due to El Niño and global warming, with the shift threatening agricultural output and straining the nation's power grids. Dry spells and sudden downpours have disrupted summer crop sowing and rural incomes. Urban areas are seeing increased power demand for cooling during monsoon pauses.

items.title: U.S. hits Iran for ninth consecutive night as American casualties rise
items.summary: The U.S. began a ninth straight night of strikes against Iran, escalating a campaign to destroy Tehran's ability to disrupt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.summary: Spain took home their first World Cup since 2010 after a 1-0 victory over 10 man Argentina.

items.summary: Suven has one of the deepest innovation pipelines in the country, solely focused on central nervous system disorders.

items.title: Centre may revive NaBFID-IIFCL merger plan after REC-PFC move to build infra financing giant
items.summary: The creation of NaBFID in 2021 came with the ambition to merge it with its older cousin IIFCL, but the plan never took off. Momentum from the ongoing merger of REC and PFC has given the plan a new life. The idea is to create a stronger DFI that can aid India's rapidly growing infra financing needs.

items.summary: Tata Technologies CEO Warren Harris says the firm's $1 billion revenue target by FY28 remains firmly on track, even though it needs a 27% CAGR, six times its recent pace, after a tariff- and EV-driven slowdown.

items.summary: ‘The unknown unknowns are where we learn,’ says Skyroot Aerospace cofounder and chief executive Pawan Kumar Chandana. India's sole space unicorn chose patience over speed by delaying its maiden commercial rocket launch by about two years, he says, adding that the success has reinforced the strategy.

items.summary: A parliamentary panel is examining changes to corporate law amendments. Investors may become partners in newly formed LLPs instead of trustees. The committee also debates proposed relaxations in corporate social responsibility norms. A framework for overseas companies to re-domicile in India is under consideration. The panel expects to adopt its report in early August for parliamentary review.

items.title: ASEAN, Africa lead India's export growth in first two months of FY27
items.summary: ASEAN and Africa were the biggest drivers of India's export growth in the first two months of FY27, with exports to ASEAN surging 66.9% to $10.5 billion and shipments to Africa rising 53.1% to $9.6 billion, according to commerce ministry data. Together, the two regions contributed over $7.6 billion in additional exports during April-May.

items.title: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
items.summary: The cost of housing is the top priority for voters ages 18 to 34, besting food and healthcare cost concerns, the CNBC All-America Economic Survey found.

items.title: iD Fresh banks on overseas markets, higher margins to become IPO-ready by Oct 2027
items.summary: Ahead of its planned IPO, iD Fresh is targeting best-in-class profitability, expanding into snacks and scaling new product launches as quick commerce reshapes India's ready-to-cook foods market.

items.title: Chinese firm seeks compensation over British Steel nationalisation
items.summary: Jingye said it will take action "through legal means to the very end" after the UK government nationalised British Steel.

items.title: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan, Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi hold "wide-ranging, productive discussions" in bilateral meeting
items.summary: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan met Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi in New Delhi. Discussions focused on strengthening trade, investment, and development partnerships between the nations. Zanzibar's President praised the new IIT Madras campus as a landmark educational initiative. External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar also met President Mwinyi to deepen bilateral ties. The visit highlights growing momentum in the India-Tanzania strategic partnership.

items.title: Samsung layoffs: Over 800 US workers affected by Texas headquarters move
items.summary: Samsung layoffs: 739 positions at Samsung Electronics America in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, were impacted as part of the company's plan to relocate its U.S. consumer electronics headquarters to Texas

items.title: PLI success prompts Centre to explore next phase for food processing sector
items.summary: The food processing ministry is reviewing lessons from the current PLI programme and consulting industry stakeholders before finalizing a new framework. It has also held discussions with PLI beneficiaries to identify segments requiring additional policy support.

items.title: US day traders flock to ‘the most dangerous product in crypto’
items.summary: Trump administration has opened American markets to highly leveraged perpetual futures

items.title: Accenture reworks salary hikes to include more employees: Splits base pay and one-time lumpsum — Here's what changes
items.summary: Accenture has reworked its salary hike composition to include more employees in the June cycle, according to a report. The new approach aims to reward more staff while managing payroll. Here's a look at what changes…

items.title: Meta layoffs: US judge rejects bid to halt AI discrimination job cuts
items.summary: A US judge has ruled that Meta can proceed with layoffs impacting 8,000 employees, despite disputes over AI usage in selecting job cuts.

items.title: Dr Reddy's Foundation soil testing lab set to hit 1-lakh sample mark ahead of 2026 rabi season
items.summary: Dr Reddy's Foundation is expanding its soil testing operations significantly. The Hyderabad lab aims to process one lakh samples before the next Rabi season. This initiative addresses concerns about depleting soil health and government scheme implementation gaps. The foundation also partners with a startup for biological soil amendments. Collaboration with ICAR-IISS will build a digital soil health repository and advanced advisories.

items.title: Late-night Argentina-Spain Fifa final fuels business for bars, hotels and delivery apps
items.summary: Extended bar timings, packed screenings and a surge in food delivery orders are turning the Fifa World Cup final into an unexpected late-night revenue opportunity for India's hospitality industry.

items.title: Afghanistan seeks deeper trade ties with India, pitches for easier business access
items.summary: Afghanistan's trade body seeks enhanced economic ties with India. They requested easier business procedures and faster visa issuance. This comes as Afghanistan diversifies trade routes away from Pakistan. India is strengthening connectivity through Iran's Chabahar port. Both nations aim for sustainable cooperation and strengthened relations.

items.title: NITI Aayog to review industrial corridor framework, seeks reforms to speed project execution
items.summary: India's government is reviewing industrial corridor frameworks to enhance project delivery. NITI Aayog will consult stakeholders on necessary policy and regulatory changes. The review aims to identify bottlenecks and recommend reforms for industrial competitiveness. This initiative supports the nation's goal of becoming a developed country by 2047.

items.title: Jio says Arpu is not under pressure. Analysts aren't so sure
items.summary: Jio says organic Arpu growth is still running at 4-5% without tariff hikes. But analysts warn the easy gains may be over, forcing telcos to look beyond connectivity for growth.

items.title: Deloitte projects 6.5-6.8% GDP expansion in FY27, growth to strengthen in second half
items.summary: Deloitte India on Sunday projected India's economy to grow between 6.5% and 6.8% in the current financial year, with momentum expected to pick up in the second half of FY27. The firm said growth would be supported by stronger festive demand, monetary easing and a gradual stabilisation in global economic conditions.

items.title: JSW Steel sees West Asia reconstruction opportunity, warns of steel import diversion
items.summary: JSW Steel expects post-war reconstruction in West Asia to create fresh demand, while warning that surplus steel from China, Japan and Russia could be diverted to India.

items.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
items.summary: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.

items.title: After a decade of falling behind, UTI AMC begins its turnaround under a new chief
items.summary: Subramaniam, who took charge in February ahead of the annual planning cycle, likens his career to football—having progressed from team captain as head of equities to manager as chief investment officer, and now to running the club as chief executive.

items.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
items.summary: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?

items.title: Oil tankers face 'worst case scenario' in Hormuz as Iran steps up attacks on ships, maritime risk CEO says
items.summary: One seafarer was killed and three were injured in attack on the crude oil tanker Al Bahyah off Oman's coast on Tuesday.

items.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
items.summary: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

items.title: If OpenAI becomes ‘Lehman Brothers of AI bubble’ – What happens to Microsoft, Nvidia, Oracle and Amazon?
items.summary: Ed Zitron claims that the AI boom is primarily driven by OpenAI, cautioning that its collapse could trigger an industry downturn. Despite heavy investments, concerns exist over profitability, particularly as OpenAI's revenue struggles to match its infrastructure costs.

items.title: SpaceX short sellers pocket $5 billion in paper profits as stock wipes out $1 trillion in value
items.summary: SpaceX's IPO generated headlines, but short sellers have reaped $5 billion in paper profits amid a stock decline. The short interest surged from $4.5 billion to $25 billion, with analysts remaining optimistic overall despite recent volatility and a market value drop to $1.63 trillion.

items.title: From Daman’s first flight to ₹28,840-cr UDAN push: India’s regional air connectivity takes off
